t_ch: 好的好的！那个…我不是按你教我的来聊天嘛，我以为你喜欢这么讲话呢。
t_en: Okay okay! Well, I'm just talking in the way you taught me to. I thought you liked this.

t_ch: 总之这事儿…多亏有你教我，我现在语气没之前那么冲了，感觉整个人都不同了呢！
t_en: Anyways... I still have to thank you. I don't talk as aggressively these days and it feels like I'm a completely new person.

t_ch: 还是不聊了…
t_en: Let's stop here...

t_ch: 还是…不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll... leave you to it.

t_ch: 嗯，没有啦没有啦，都还好的。嗯嗯！
t_en: No no. I'm all good. I really am!

t_ch: 虚伪之人，罪不可恕，千刀万剐，死不足惜！
t_en: Liars are unforgivable! Even death cannot redeem them!

t_ch: 哼，很快我就可以写完这份报告书了…卡波特，你等着吧！
t_en: Hmph, I'm going to finish writing this report very soon... Just you wait, Capote!

t_ch: 你不是波少头号粉丝吗？
t_en: Aren't you Master Capote's biggest fan?

t_ch: 呸呸呸！世玲与你无冤无仇，你怎么能用这么晦气的名号来称呼世玲呢？
t_en: Bah! I've never wronged you, so don't give me a title that brings bad luck!

t_ch: 脱粉即无关，希望你可以尊重世玲！
t_en: It's all over once I stopped being his fan. Please show me some respect!

t_ch: 卡波特做了什么？
t_en: What did Capote do?

t_ch: 你什么记性呀？当时你不也在场嘛！哎呀，世玲可不愿意再回忆一次痛苦的经历。
t_en: Don't you remember? You were there too! Ugh, I don't want to relive that terrible experience ever again.

t_ch: 总之，你只要记住卡波特是个大坏蛋就对了！
t_en: Anyways, just remember that Capote is despicable!

t_ch: 我还有些事想问你…
t_en: There's something else I want to ask.

t_ch: 你在写什么报告书？
t_en: What report are you writing?

t_ch: 当然是统计卡波特罪行的报告书呀！作为他之前的头号粉丝，世玲可是知道许多秘密的…那些事儿，旁人想都不敢想！
t_en: A report that summarizes Capote's sins! I know a lot of secrets as his former top fan... Other people can't even imagine it!

t_ch: 可惜写作进度太慢了，都怪阿德勒每天来烦我，不然世玲早就写完发到空间站的匿名论坛里去了。
t_en: Pity that I'm writing so slowly. I blame Adler — he comes to bother me every day, otherwise I'd have finished this ages ago and published it on the space station's anonymous forum.

t_ch: 波少粉丝后援会解散了吗？
t_en: Did the Master Capote Fan Club disband?

t_ch: 当然解散啦！大家逃都来不及呢。只不过听说还有少部分人还打算继续支持他，说什么「要陪伴波少洗心革面」。
t_en: Of course it did! We couldn't get away fast enough. But I hear some people plan to keep supporting him and want to "be there for him as he changes."

t_ch: 真是不理解那帮人！像卡波特这样的坏东西还有什么值得关心的？搞不懂，真搞不懂！
t_en: I don't get those people at all! People like Capote don't deserve any care! I don't get it. I really don't!

t_ch: 那些你给卡波特的奇物…
t_en: Those Curios you gave Capote...

t_ch: 嘘！！亏世玲还把你当朋友，什么话都愿意给你讲，你怎么稍不留心就把世玲的秘密大声讲出来了？！这要是让旁人听去了怎么办！？
t_en: Shhh! I told you that because we are friends, but that doesn't mean you can say it out loud! What if other people hear?

t_ch: 那些东西世玲早就安排对应负责人进行回收了，你别瞎操心。这件事就当翻页了！以后不准再提了！
t_en: I already got the relevant parties to take back those Curios. Don't you worry about it. Just treat it as water under the bridge! Don't mention it ever again!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 等着瞧吧卡波特！等我忙完手里的工作，整理完奇物报告，再去忙完收容回收检查，最后再和阿德勒去一趟…哎哟！世玲怎么还有那么多事要忙呀！
t_en: Just you wait, Capote! When I finished my work, tidied up the Curio report, supervised Curio reclamation, and went with Adler to... Ugh! How come I still have so much work to do!?

t_ch: 首先去验收收容回来的奇物，然后再去做例行仓库抽检，最后答应了要陪阿德勒去找那个会说话的怪东西…哎哟，今天也没空写报告了！
t_en: Firstly I need to do the inventory for reclaimed Curios, then do regular quality control at the warehouse, then I promised Adler to go find the weird thing that talks... Great, no time to write my report today either!

t_ch: 我还有些事想问你…
t_en: There's something else I want to ask.

t_ch: 你在写什么报告书？
t_en: What report are you writing?

t_ch: 当然是统计卡波特罪行的报告书呀！作为他之前的头号粉丝，世玲可是知道许多秘密的…那些事儿，旁人想都不敢想！
t_en: A report that summarizes Capote's sins! I know a lot of secrets as his former top fan... Other people can't even imagine it!

t_ch: 可惜写作进度太慢了，都怪阿德勒每天来烦我，不然世玲早就写完发到空间站的匿名论坛里去了。
t_en: Pity that I'm writing so slowly. I blame Adler — he comes to bother me every day, otherwise I'd have finished this ages ago and published it on the space station's anonymous forum.

t_ch: 波少粉丝后援会解散了吗？
t_en: Did the Master Capote Fan Club disband?

t_ch: 当然解散啦！大家逃都来不及呢。只不过听说还有少部分人还打算继续支持他，说什么「要陪伴波少洗心革面」。
t_en: Of course it did! We couldn't get away fast enough. But I hear some people plan to keep supporting him and want to "be there for him as he changes."

t_ch: 真是不理解那帮人！像卡波特这样的混账东西还有什么值得关心的？搞不懂，真搞不懂！
t_en: I don't get those people at all! Scoundrels like Capote don't deserve any care! I don't get it. I really don't!

t_ch: 那些你给卡波特的奇物…
t_en: Those Curios you gave Capote...

t_ch: 嘘！！亏世玲还把你当朋友，什么话都愿意给你讲，你怎么稍不留心就把世玲的秘密大声讲出来了？！这要是让旁人听去了怎么办！？
t_en: Shhh! I told you that because we are friends, but that doesn't mean you can say it out loud! What if other people hear?

t_ch: 那些东西世玲早就安排对应负责人进行回收了，你别瞎操心。这件事就当翻页了！以后不准再提了！
t_en: I already got the relevant parties to take back those Curios. Don't you worry about it. Just treat it as water under the bridge! Don't mention it ever again!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 等着瞧吧卡波特！等我忙完手里的工作，整理完奇物报告，再去忙完收容回收检查，最后再和阿德勒去一趟…哎哟！世玲怎么还有那么多事要忙呀！
t_en: Just you wait, Capote! When I finished my work, tidied up the Curio report, supervised Curio reclamation, and went with Adler to... Ugh! How come I still have so much work to do!?

t_ch: 哈欠——困死了。啊…又是你，这次又是什么事？
t_en: *Yawn* I'm so sleepy. You again? What's up?

t_ch: 我有一些问题想要问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 你又在偷懒？
t_en: You are slacking off again?

t_ch: …什么叫偷懒？我是医疗科的成员乔安，每天要处理的事情很多，不抽空休息会儿还得了…我这副小身板又不是铁打的。
t_en: ...What do you mean, slacking off? I'm Joanne from the Medical Department, and I've got a lot to do every day. I need time to relax... otherwise my body won't be able to handle it.

t_ch: 听说你之前一直没休息过？
t_en: I heard you didn't rest for a long time?

t_ch: 哎，别提了，昼夜连轴转，最后直接累趴下了。
t_en: Ooh, don't get me started. I'd been working <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7 before I burnt out.

t_ch: 既然你知道这事，就别跟我搭话了，我得休息会儿……
t_en: If you knew this, then just leave me alone. I need to rest...

t_ch: 打扰一个想打瞌睡的人，比打扰一个正在熟睡的人更加恶劣。
t_en: It's worse to disturb someone trying to sleep than wake up someone already asleep.

t_ch: 听说你感染了模因病毒？
t_en: So, you got the "memetic virus"?

t_ch: …连你都听说了啊？肯定是咱们医疗科那几个大嘴巴到处说。
t_en: ...Even you are in the know? It must be those blabbermouths at the medical department.

t_ch: 哎，算我倒霉，我也不知道怎么就感染这玩意。
t_en: Ugh, this is so unfair. I have no idea how I even got this darn thing.

t_ch: 好在因祸得福，让我休息了几天，总算是睡了个好觉……
t_en: Mercifully, it turned out to be a blessing in disguise. I got to take a few days off, and finally got a good night's sleep...

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I better leave you alone then.

t_ch: 那还是不打扰你了。
t_en: Well, then, I'd better leave you alone.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，你自便吧，我得休息了，一会儿还要继续看诊呢。
t_en: Sure, you go ahead. I need to get some rest before my next shift.

t_ch: 小年轻，又见面了。坐坐坐，别见外。
t_en: Kiddo, we meet again. Take a seat.

t_ch: 我想多听听你扯闲篇…
t_en: I want to hear more of your gossip...

t_ch: 有没有什么陈年旧梦的八卦？
t_en: Are there any gossips from the old days?

t_ch: 这个话题我有发言权。
t_en: Oh, I am best qualified to talk about this.

t_ch: 伯纳德你认识吧？就是地概科那个倔老头，一看就不好惹， 眉毛比眼睛都有精神的那个。
t_en: You know Bernard, don't you? The stubborn old guy from the Department of Cosmic Geography who doesn't look very approachable. His eyebrows are fiercer than his eyes.

t_ch: 你别看他现在这么阻挠他徒弟的爱情，当年的他，好家伙，那叫一个多情郎啊。
t_en: Don't be fooled by his seeming determination to prevent his apprentice from dating. You never could've guessed that he used to be quite the womanizer back in his younger days.

t_ch: 年轻的时候吧，这伯纳德情窦初开，想要追密卷科一个姑娘。我们这些做兄弟的能不帮忙吗？那前前后后，忙了好一哆嗦。
t_en: Back when he was young, Bernard fell in love for the first time with a girl from the Department of Insight. As his brothers, we all helped him to pursue her for a while.

t_ch: 这年轻时候的爱情就是炽热，我怀疑伯纳德那老东西，就是在洛奇身上，看到了自个儿年轻时候的影子吧，嘿。
t_en: Young love is always full of passion. I guess Bernard just saw his younger self in Rocky, haha.

t_ch: 啊？你问我之后怎么着了？年轻人，这听八卦可不兴一口气听完。想要知道，就请听下回分解吧！
t_en: What's that? You want to know what happened then? Kiddo, I can't spill all the beans in one go. Stay tuned!

t_ch: 有没有什么难以忘怀的故事？
t_en: Any unforgettable stories?

t_ch: 到了我这把岁数看过去，那啥事都多少有点难以忘怀，你懂这意思吧？
t_en: At my age, everything is unforgettable. You know what I mean?

t_ch: 真不是我说，你们年轻人其实不爱听这句，但我还是得说——没有啥忘不掉的事儿，没有啥过不去的坎儿！真要是有，那就是当时没琢磨明白，当局者迷了！
t_en: I know young people these days hate to hear this, but nothing is truly unforgettable, and there's nothing you can't get over! If there is, it's only because you've been too lost to figure it out!

t_ch: 被你这么一问，我脑子里还真糊涂起来了。一下子好多事儿都冲出来了。真是不能提，不能想啊。
t_en: Now that you brought it up, I am actually confused. I just remembered so many things all at once. The past really doesn't bear being remembered.

t_ch: 什么古恩那家伙年轻的时候风风火火的样子，还有伯纳德刚来的时候那身板才叫一个结实……
t_en: How energetic Gunn used to be... And how strong Bernard was when he first joined us...

t_ch: 哎，物是人非咯！
t_en: *sigh* How things have changed!

t_ch: 你最近都在忙什么呢？
t_en: What have you been working on lately?

t_ch: 退休之后就不能说自己在忙什么了，一般都说是：最近在玩儿着什么呢。
t_en: I'm retired, so that might not be an accurate question. We normally just say what we've been playing with.

t_ch: 我没啥文化，不像是古恩看得明白愚人戏。我大半辈子都搞机修，和载具称兄道弟，老了也只会折腾点这个。
t_en: I never got a proper education. I wouldn't understand Fool's Play like Gunn does. My life's work has been all about repairing and handling machines and vehicles, and now that I'm old, that's still the only thing I do.

t_ch: 喏，瞧见没，这个小玩具。可别觉得是给小孩儿玩的！这辆装甲车跑过的路，比我喝的酒都多！这个小模型，里头完美复刻了原型的机动轨道呢。
t_en: Here, check this out. Don't think this is some child's plaything! The armored car this miniature model is based on has traveled on more roads than I could count! This model even has a perfect replica of the real thing's mechanized track.

t_ch: 我当年可是修了它足足三次，三次都是从濒死边缘把它救回来……
t_en: I fixed this thing three times. Three times I managed to bring it back from the brink of death ...

t_ch: 哎哟，不说了不说了，都是过去的事儿咯！
t_en: Well, I think I'm done talking. These stories are all water under the bridge.

t_ch: 没什么别的想问了。
t_en: No other questions.

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 和你扯闲篇，够劲儿，开心！年轻人，下次有空再来，别的没有，故事管够！
t_en: It was fun chatting with you. I liked it! Come here again when you have time, kiddo. I might not have much in my possession, but I sure have more than enough stories to share.

t_ch: 哎，是你啊，来找老头子是有什么事吗？
t_en: Ah, it's you again. How can an old man like me help you?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to know...

t_ch: 有洛奇的下落了吗？
t_en: Any news about Rocky's whereabouts?

t_ch: 哼，这个没良心的小子，早把老头子我忘咯！别说下落了，连个消息都没给我发，亏我还天天刷新终端看有没有未读消息……
t_en: Hmph, that ungrateful brat has long forgotten about me. He never sent me any messages, let alone his whereabouts. And here I am, logging onto the terminal every day to see whether there are any unread messages...

t_ch: 不过…虽然没收到他的消息，但他的生命体征信号一直是亮着的，显示正常，多半没啥事。
t_en: But... his vital signs always appear normal based on the reading. He's probably okay.

t_ch: 可能是那边信号不太好吧。
t_en: Maybe he's got a bad signal where he is.

t_ch: 洛奇走后，你还好吗？
t_en: Is everything all right without Rocky around?

t_ch: 哼，别提了！刚刚知道洛奇走了的时候，可差点没把我气死！
t_en: Ugh, don't even get me started. I was absolutely livid when I first realized Rocky had left.

t_ch: 好在我去医疗科做了个检查，老头子我身子骨硬朗得很，别的不敢说，等这傻小子回来是足够了！
t_en: Thankfully, my results from the Medical Department are pretty good, and I feel I'm in good enough health to wait for this silly boy's return.

t_ch: 其他人知道洛奇的事吗?
t_en: Does anyone else know about this?

t_ch: 反正我那傻徒弟不在空间站里，别人知不知道都无所谓了。
t_en: It doesn't matter, my silly apprentice is not in the space station anyway.

t_ch: 而且…就算知道了又有什么用呢？又不见谁去把他拽回来……
t_en: Besides, what use would that be even if they knew? It's not like they're gonna drag Rocky back...

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 那我还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you alone.

t_ch: 哎，洛奇啊，我的傻徒弟…师父早就不怪你了，可你什么时候才会回来哇……
t_en: Ahhh, Rocky, my silly apprentice... I've never blamed you for leaving, but when are you coming back...?

t_ch: …咳咳。不好意思，失态了。我刚才什么都没说。
t_en: ...Ahem, sorry. Excuse me. Just pretend I didn't say anything.

t_ch: 谢谢你邀请我登上列车。我就自己随意转转吧，不用劳您大驾专门陪我了。
t_en: Thank you for inviting me to board the Express. I'll take a look around on my own. You don't have to accompany me.

t_ch: 真不用我带着你转转？
t_en: You don't want me to show you around?

t_ch: 真不用，让我一个人静静吧。再见！
t_en: You really don't have to. I'd like to hang out on my own. Bye!

t_ch: 查阅了你们列车的智库之后，我才意识到，我们贝洛伯格拥有的知识与你们相比，实在是不值一提……
t_en: After going through the Express' data bank, I've truly realized just how little knowledge Belobog possesses compared to you...

t_ch: 如今贝洛伯格正逐渐恢复正常，也在逐步取得与外界的联系。为了避免潜在的敌人对我们造成威胁，我必须尽可能把你们的知识全都带回去才行。
t_en: Now that Belobog is gradually returning to normal, we are also slowly reconnecting with the outside world. I have to take as much of your knowledge back with me as possible to prevent any potential enemies from endangering us.

t_ch: …当然，我会尽量不影响丹恒的，放心吧。
t_en: ...Don't worry, I'll try my best not to bother Dan Heng.

t_ch: 列车智库随时欢迎你。
t_en: The Express' data bank welcomes you at all times.

t_ch: 哼哼，那我可要整天泡在这里了。
t_en: Really? Then I'm gonna spend all day here.

t_ch: …开个玩笑。毕竟丹恒还得睡觉呢。
t_en: ...I'm kidding. Dan Heng needs the room to sleep in, after all.

t_ch: 看到了什么有趣的东西？
t_en: See anything interesting?

t_ch: 嗯…挺多的。让我比较在意的是那个叫作「星际和平公司」的商业组织……
t_en: Quite a lot. I'm most interested in that commercial organization — the Interastral Peace Corporation.

t_ch: 明明都是克里珀的追随者，他们的做法与我们筑城者却大相径庭——说到底，做买卖真能支援「存护」吗？我在怀疑他们的真实意图为何……
t_en: They are also followers of Qlipoth, but what they do is drastically different from us Architects. Can businessmen honestly support the Preservation? I'm seriously doubting their true motives...

t_ch: 你不会要全部背下来吧…
t_en: You're not gonna memorize it all, right...?

t_ch: 不然呢，用纸笔抄写吗？这么多内容，我一个人可搬不动。
t_en: How else can I take it back? By copying it on paper? I can't carry that much paper on my own.

t_ch: 而且，手机的存储容量也完全不够…所以还是用人脑记忆来得方便些。
t_en: And my phone definitely doesn't have enough storage... so, memorizing it is the most convenient way to go.

t_ch: 话说回来，我还意识到一件事——你们智库的加密手段实在是有些薄弱……
t_en: Actually, I realized something else — the encryption for your data bank is pretty weak...

t_ch: …这可不是小问题，稍后我会和姬子小姐聊聊的。
t_en: ...That could be a big issue. I'll have a chat with Himeko about it.

t_ch: 我查阅了列车智库……
t_en: I checked the Express Data Bank...

t_ch: 查阅智库的时候，我还意识到一件事——你们智库的加密手段实在是有些薄弱……
t_en: I realized something else when going through the data bank — the encryption for your data bank is pretty weak...

t_ch: …这可不是小问题，稍后我会和姬子小姐聊聊的。
t_en: ...That could be a big issue. I'll have a chat with Himeko about it.

t_ch: 此前与姬子小姐聊天，我这才了解到雅利洛-Ⅵ并非唯一因为星核蒙受灾难的星球，宇宙中遭遇了同样命运的世界远超我们想象……
t_en: It was only after talking to Himeko that I learned that Jarilo-VI isn't the only planet suffering from the Stellaron's calamity. The number of worlds that endured such a fate far surpasses what we imagined...

t_ch: 尽管如此，姬子小姐还是向我表示，星核的成因至今为止仍是个谜，因此目前没有切实可行的办法消除星核带来的污染。
t_en: Even so, Himeko indicated that the source of Stellarons remains mysterious, and there are no realistic ways to remove a Stellaron's contamination.

t_ch: 不过，她也向我分享了其他星球采取的对策——其中有些案例与我们的状况很是相似，我正在考虑和杰帕德戍卫官讨论采取类似对策的可行性……
t_en: However, she shared some strategies that other planets have taken — some of them are very similar to our circumstances. I'm considering discussing with Captain Gepard the possibilities of adopting those strategies...

t_ch: 可以试试看。
t_en: Nothing to lose.

t_ch: 是啊，我也觉得有值得一试的价值。
t_en: I agree. They're at least worth a try.

t_ch: 也许没什么用。
t_en: Maybe they won't be of much use.

t_ch: 我也没有太大把握——但无论如何，我想有值得一试的价值。
t_en: I'm not confident either — but they're at least worth a try.

t_ch: 你说话听起来好复杂…
t_en: That was really complicated...

t_ch: …咳咳，不好意思。可能是我的职业病犯了。
t_en: ...Ahem, sorry, I was in work mode.

t_ch: 总之，我想说的是——我们正在想办法消除星核和裂界带来的影响。
t_en: Anyways, what I'm trying to say is that we're seeking ways to remove the influence of the Stellaron and the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 我们铁卫已经与裂界对抗了太久，付出了太大的代价…哪怕是星核问题已经被你们解决了的当下，伤亡报告上的数字还是令人触目惊心。
t_en: Us Silvermane Guards have fought against the Fragmentum for far too long and have lost too much... Even though you've solved the Stellaron problem for now, the number of casualties is still staggering.

t_ch: 如果，我是说如果——如果我们真的能够找到根除裂界污染的方法——那么，我，还有杰帕德，就再也不必眼睁睁地看着战友们白白送命……
t_en: If — and I mean if — we can truly find a way to uproot the Fragmentum's corruption, then maybe we can stem the Silvermane Guards' losses...

t_ch: …啊，对不起，这话题好像有点太沉重了。请让我一个人静一静吧，谢谢你。
t_en: ...Ah, sorry... this topic always makes me a little emotional. I think I need some alone time — thank you.

t_ch: 该和杰帕德讨论一下了……
t_en: Time for a chat with Gepard...

t_ch: 如果我们真的能够找到根除裂界污染的方法……
t_en: If we can truly find a way to uproot the Fragmentum's corruption...

t_ch: …大家应该都会感到欣慰吧。
t_en: ...a great weight would be lifted.

t_en: I didn't think that black drink would taste so good... and really energizing too! If only I could get a cup when I'm pulling those all-nighters...

t_ch: 其实，单看卖相我还以为根本不能喝呢。大千世界真是无奇不有啊。
t_en: Judging from its appearance, I thought it'd be unpalatable. The wonders of the universe are endless.

t_en: Do you mean... Jim Roger?

t_ch: 我说的是咖啡——应该是叫这个名字吧？
t_en: I'm talking about coffee — is that what it's called?

t_en: Do you mean... Himeko's coffee?

t_ch: 对，就是咖啡。

t_en: Himeko gave me a big handful of coffee beans when she saw how much I liked it — and then she gave me a coffee machine! It's a bit embarrassing, hehe.

t_ch: 下次再来列车做客的话，一定要给她带些贝洛伯格特产作回礼才行。
t_en: Next time I visit the Express, I'll definitely bring her some Belobog specialties in return.

t_ch: 试喝了姬子小姐的饮料……

t_ch: 下次再来列车做客的话，一定要给她带些贝洛伯格特产作回礼才行。
t_en: Next time I visit the Express, I'll definitely bring her some Belobog specialties in return.

t_en: I've been thinking since the last camping trip that it would be great if I can go somewhere even further than the Snow Plains.

t_en: That thought has been growing stronger ever since I boarded the Express... and saw views that would never have been visible from the city or the Snow Plains.

t_en: Don't worry. I'm not here for you. I'm here to find March and discuss some... opinions about books, and I brought you a small gift. Here. Don't misunderstand. I'm definitely not here for you.

t_en: Mt. Zarathustrazapala Iskandra — it's the tallest mountain we know of on the planet...

t_en: ...Before I boarded the Express, I thought Belobog and the Snow Plains were the entire world. I didn't think reality would surpass my imagination this much.

t_ch: 继续探索下去吧。
t_en: Never stop exploring.

t_en: I asked him how to use the Express' data bank... but somehow we ended up talking about Belobog geography.

t_ch: 然后，我们又聊了很多其他星球的自然地理——虽然只是我一直在单方面地听他讲…但那些知识都非常有趣。
t_en: Then we talked about the geography of lots of other planets. Well, he talked and I listened... but it was very interesting.

t_en: I never thought the world beyond Belobog could be so huge...

t_ch: …真期待以后的天外探险啊。

t_en: I've always lived in the Underworld and never ever imagined... I just can't believe I can look at my home from this angle...

t_ch: 听说雅洛…啊不，应该是雅利洛-VI，对吧？听说雅利洛-VI以前不是白茫茫的一片，还有各种各样的地形跟自然环境。那些画面实在是神奇，靠我自己根本想象不出来。
t_en: I heard Jalo... Uhh I mean Jarilo — right? I heard Jarilo-VI didn't use to be all snowy like this, and it had all sorts of landscapes and natural environments. They are totally magical to me. I'd never have imagined it myself.

t_ch: 指望它变回以前的样子是不是不太现实？不过人总得有点梦想嘛，哈哈哈~
t_en: Maybe it's too unrealistic to wish it'd go back to the way it was, but it's good to have a dream, right? Hahaha~

t_ch: 没有什么是不可能的。
t_en: Nothing is impossible.

t_ch: 噢，这话我爱听！虽然我也想去更有生机的地方看两眼，但那些地方毕竟不是自己的家乡，你说是吧？
t_en: Yeah, that's the spirit! I wanna see some places with a little more get up and go, y'know? Still, those places wouldn't be home.

t_ch: …比如现在，我才刚来没一会儿，就已经有点怀念下层区空气的味道了。哈哈哈~
t_en: ...Case in point, I've only been here for a while, but I'm already kinda missing the taste of the Underworld air. Hahaha!

t_ch: 哈，真壮观！
t_en: Wow, how magnificent!

t_ch: 要是能通过自己的双手，让雅利洛-VI回到鼎盛时的样子…想想就让人热血沸腾！
t_en: If I could find a way to turn the clock back on Jarilo-VI... Just thinking about it makes me excited!

t_ch: 宇宙旅行当然很有吸引力，不过我最想做的事…果然还是帮助自己身边的人，守护家园。
t_en: Space travel is pretty tempting, but what I wanna do most... is help the people around me and protect my home.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，来得正好！我正想给列车组提提意见呢。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're here right on time! I was about to give some feedback to the Express Crew.

t_ch: 这列车上的餐食，分量实在太小了！下层区条件最差的时候，也不见小吃摊老板这么抠抠搜搜的啊。
t_en: Uh... The portion sizes on the Express are too small! Things were pretty scarce in the Underworld, but the food stall owners were never this stingy.

t_ch: 也有可能是你食量太大…
t_en: Maybe you have a big appetite...

t_ch: 唔，是吗？
t_en: Um. Really?

t_ch: 可能也有道理，毕竟搏击手每天的摄入可比普通人要大多了，哈哈哈。
t_en: Maybe that's true. A boxer does need to take in a lot more energy than the average person. Hahaha.

t_ch: 少量多餐是健康的吃法。
t_en: It's healthier to eat more meals in smaller amounts.

t_ch: 唔，这么讲究？
t_en: Um, that's a thing?

t_ch: 我反正不太习惯。吃饭嘛，就是要敞开肚皮才够劲！
t_en: I'm not really used to that. Eating is all about eating your fill!

t_ch: 还有…呃，我发现，列车长给的盒饭里总会掺些奇奇怪怪的蔬菜…不知道，反正我是吃不惯。
t_en: And, uhh... it seems like the conductor likes to include these weird vegetables in the lunches... I don't know what they are, but I don't like them.

t_ch: 哎，要不是为了保证营养摄入，我宁愿顿顿都点大宇宙炒饭……
t_en: *sigh* If I didn't have to worry about nutrition, I'd be ordering Cosmic Fried Rice every meal...

t_ch: 这列车上的饭……
t_en: The meals on the Express...

t_ch: 列车上的饭，分量也太少了！
t_en: The portion sizes on the Express are too small!

t_ch: 有机会的话，得把我的意见转告给列车长……
t_en: When I get the chance, I gotta give the conductor my feedback...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我正在思考一件事，你听听有没有道理——
t_en: Hey {NICKNAME}, I was thinking about something. Hear it out and see if it makes sense —

t_ch: 你看，我这只机械手是下层区的工匠帮忙打造的，用上的技术在贝洛伯格也算尖端了。
t_en: See, my mechanical hand is made by the craftsmen in the Underworld. The techniques they used are the best in Belobog.

t_ch: 可我上了列车以后才发现，你们这儿日常用的普通设备，都比我以前见过的各种科技发明要先进多了！
t_en: But after I boarded the Express, I found out that even your most ordinary everyday equipment is way more advanced than any tech I've ever seen!

t_ch: 要是有可能的话…我是不是该找谁帮我改造下这只义手，让它更有威力？
t_en: I wonder... should I ask somebody here to modify my hand a bit and make it more powerful?

t_ch: 比如装个激光炮？
t_en: Like giving it a laser cannon?

t_ch: …这个点子好！
t_en: ...That's a good idea!

t_ch: 有了大威力的武器，让我一次对付一百个裂界怪物都是小事一桩！
t_en: With such a powerful weapon, I'd be able to take on a hundred Fragmentum monsters at once!

t_ch: 搏击手不是只靠拳头吗…
t_en: Aren't boxers supposed to rely on their fists...?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，当然不是用在拳台上了。
t_en: Hahaha, of course I'm not gonna use it in the ring.

t_ch: 你想啊，如果能给这只手加上大威力的武器…那我一次应付一百个裂界怪物肯定都不成问题！
t_en: Just think about it. If I had a weapon that powerful in the palm of my hand, I could take on a hundred Fragmentum monsters at once!

t_ch: 很好，就这么决定了！我晚点就去找姬子小姐聊聊。
t_en: That's it then! Cool! I'll go chat with Himeko about this later.

t_ch: 为什么是姬子？
t_en: Why Himeko?

t_ch: …嗯？因为姬子小姐看上去就很懂武器，枪啊炮啊什么的。
t_en: ...Why not? Seems like Himeko knows a lot about weapons — guns, cannons, you name it!

t_ch: 唔，我的直觉不对吗？
t_en: Uhh, that is unless you're thinking of a different Himeko?

t_ch: 如果有这么高级的技术……
t_en: If only such advanced tech were available...

t_ch: 晚点我就去找姬子小姐聊聊，看看她有没有什么改装机械手的建议。
t_en: I'll go chat with Himeko later and see if she has any advice about modifying my mechanical hand.

t_ch: 我一眼就能看出来，她对武器改装肯定特别在行！
t_en: I knew from the moment I saw her that she must be really good at modifying weapons!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！我想问问，列车上有训练室吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}! Do you know if there's a training room on the Express?

t_ch: 没有…
t_en: No...

t_ch: 没有？这可不行啊……
t_en: No? That won't do...

t_ch: 我也不知道。
t_en: I don't actually know.

t_ch: 咦？你们平时不训练吗？
t_en: Huh? Don't you train?

t_ch: 我也希望有。
t_en: I wish there was one.

t_ch: …就是没有的意思？这可不行啊……
t_en: ...So you don't have one? That won't do...

t_ch: 我看你们也经常要和险恶的敌人作战，不进行常规化的体能训练怎么行！
t_en: I figured you folks fight nasty enemies all the time. How can you not have regular physical training?

t_ch: 决定了，我要去和列车长提意见，给你们改造一间训练房出来！
t_en: I've decided. I'm gonna raise the issue with the conductor and get them to put together a training room for you!

t_ch: 训练…训练。
t_en: Training... Training...

t_ch: 你们应该经常要和险恶的敌人交战吧？为了能用最佳状态迎敌，常规化的体能训练可是必备项啊。
t_en: You folks fight nasty enemies all the time, right? You need to have regular physical training to stay in top form.

t_ch: 决定了，我要去和列车长提意见，给你们改造一间训练房出来！
t_en: I've decided. I'm gonna raise the issue with the conductor and get them to put together a training room for you!

t_ch: 唷，{NICKNAME}！精神不错嘛。
t_en: Yo, yo, {NICKNAME}! Looking good.

t_ch: 我刚刚碰到三月七和丹恒了——之前只听说过他们跟你并肩作战的事，现在终于有机会见见本人了。
t_en: I just met March and Dan Heng — I'd heard about your adventures together but this is the first chance I got to meet them in the flesh.

t_ch: 真是两个有趣的家伙，哈哈哈！有他们陪着，旅途肯定不会无聊吧？
t_en: Interesting duo, haha! Guess they must keep you pretty entertained, huh?

t_ch: 我觉得你和三月七能合得来。
t_en: I thought you'd get along well with March 7th.

t_ch: 三月小姐，性格真是活泼！
t_en: March has a lot of energy!

t_ch: 师傅和希儿都嫌我话痨，真没想到还能撞见话匣子比我还难关上的人，哈哈哈！
t_en: My master and Seele both think that I talk too much. Never thought I'd run into someone chattier than me... hahaha!

t_ch: 我觉得你和丹恒能做朋友。
t_en: I feel like you and Dan Heng could be good friends.

t_ch: 真的吗？我觉得他好像不太爱说话……
t_en: Really? I get the feeling he doesn't like to talk...

t_ch: 师傅和希儿都嫌我话痨，不知道丹恒小哥是不是也这么想？
t_en: My master and Seele both think that I talk too much. I wonder if Dan Heng thinks the same.

t_ch: 能跟这么有趣的人一起旅行，真是让人羡慕！
t_en: I'm jealous that you get to travel with such interesting people!

t_ch: 你可得好好珍惜自己的朋友啊，{NICKNAME}——糟了，我刚才的语气是不是跟奥列格师傅一模一样？
t_en: You should treasure your friends, {NICKNAME}... Uh-oh, am I starting to sound like master Oleg?

t_ch: 三月和丹恒……
t_en: March and Dan Heng...

t_ch: 能跟三月和丹恒这么有趣的伙伴一起旅行，可真是让人羡慕。
t_en: I'm jealous that you get to travel with people as interesting as March and Dan Heng!

t_ch: 记得好好珍惜自己身边的朋友，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: You should treasure your friends, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，快来！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, this way!

t_ch: 我之前整理东西、翻箱倒柜的时候找到了些多余的物资，说不定对你们有用。
t_en: I found some extra supplies while I was tidying up — might be a help to you.

t_ch: 下层区最近太平得很，用不上这些东西。所以我寻思还不如分给列车组，指不定能派上些用场。
t_en: It's been pretty peaceful in the Underworld recently, so we don't have a lot of use for this stuff anymore. I figured I might as well share it all with the Express Crew.

t_ch: 拿去吧，别客气！
t_en: Take it! Don't be shy!

t_ch: 我手上有些东西……
t_en: I have something with me...

t_ch: 既然列车组帮下层区渡过了劫难，那我也得尽力回馈你们才行。
t_en: Since the Express Crew helped the Underworld through such a tough time, I need to do my best to help you in return.

t_ch: 所谓朋友不就该这样吗？哈哈哈！
t_en: Isn't that what being friends is all about? Hahaha!

t_ch: 你们这列车上的风景竟还不错。本座久居这太卜司中，囿于洞天中虚假的星空，竟有些忘记了真实世界的壮丽。
t_en: The sights from this Express of yours are surprisingly pleasant. I've lived within the Divination Commission under the delve's artificial sky for so long that I'd somewhat forgotten the magnificence of the real world.

t_ch: 你喜欢就好。
t_en: I'm glad you like it.

t_ch: 欢迎你常来。
t_en: You're welcome to visit more often.

t_ch: 本座也时常想去那些遐方绝域，见识一下那些盛景。毕竟，世之奇伟瑰怪非常之观，常在于险远。
t_en: I hope to visit more of such marvelous places and to gaze upon such views with my own eyes... The most grandiose, astounding, and extraordinary vistas are often found in perilous and distant lands.

t_ch: 可惜罗浮一天也离不开我…就算想去哪里看看，他们也不愿放行吧。
t_en: It's a pity that the Luofu cannot part from me for even a day... Even if I wished to visit other lands, they probably wouldn't allow it.

t_ch: 这样说来，还要感谢你们…有时公务繁忙，还可以来这里躲个清闲…你们是仙舟的贵客，自也没人敢来这里找我。
t_en: I must thank you for this... I can come here to have some peace and quiet when my work gets too busy... You are esteemed guests on the Xianzhou — I doubt any would dare to pull me away.

t_ch: 这等方寸洞天，竟还有如此风景…真是不错，甚得我心。
t_en: Such a minute place, and yet such splendid scenery... just splendid.

t_ch: 风景还不错……
t_en: The view is not bad...

t_ch: 这等方寸洞天，竟还有如此风景…真是不错，甚得我心。
t_en: Such a minute place, and yet such splendid scenery... just splendid.

t_ch: 不知不觉，本座竟与姬子闲谈了半日…
t_en: I didn't realize I'd spent half a day conversing with Himeko...

t_ch: 能与博学之人闲谈足足半日，真是人生的一大乐事。
t_en: It is indeed a pleasure to converse with such a knowledgeable peer for so long.

t_ch: 你们聊了什么？
t_en: What did you talk about?

t_ch: 倒也没什么…和姬子谈了谈列车上引航系统的算法迭代。
t_en: Nothing too complex... we discussed upgrading the algorithms in the Express' navigation system.

t_ch: 我们都认为，当前的算法并非最优解，也都认为各自对阿僧祇连续统能做出更好的效果。
t_en: We agree that the current algorithms are sub-optimal, and that the Asamkhyeya Continuum can produce better results.

t_ch: 不过，在具体实施方案上，我们产生了一些分歧。
t_en: However, we had a difference of opinion regarding the execution.

t_ch: 姬子认为，采取宇宙际玛姬诺夫理论，可以迭代出最佳的优化模型。
t_en: Himeko thinks the Interstellar Maginov Theory can iterate the most perfect optimization model.

t_ch: 我倒是觉得，詹姆斯-真贺田体系本身就存在问题，以此为根基的形式体系也必然在可靠性上存在缺陷。
t_en: However, I feel the James-Magata System contains fundamental issues, causing any formal systems derived from it to inevitably contain irregularities.

t_ch: 最后我们决定在模拟机上测试一下…结果是，姬子设想的算法更为高效，可以大幅优化列车引航系统的计算精度。
t_en: We finally decided to test them on an emulator... It turns out Himeko's proposed calculations are more efficient, and will significantly improve the accuracy of the Express' navigational system.

t_ch: 从结论而言，是本座败了…不过真理嘛，并不在于口舌胜败。本座依然很满足。
t_en: As such, Himeko prevailed... But the truth isn't about winning or losing. I am content.

t_ch: 你刚刚说了什么？
t_en: ...Could you repeat that again?

t_ch: 我一句都没听懂…
t_en: I didn't get any of that...

t_ch: 无妨，纯粹理性的乐趣所在，注定不为所有人理解。
t_en: No matter. It is the joy of pure logic, destined not to be comprehended by all.

t_ch: 与姬子闲谈了半日…
t_en: I chatted with Himeko for hours...

t_ch: 纯粹理性的乐趣所在，注定不为所有人理解。
t_en: It is the joy of pure logic, destined not to be comprehended by all.

t_ch: 你们列车上那种域外的象棋，你玩过吗？
t_en: Have you tried the strange chess game you have here on the Express?

t_ch: 还没有诶…
t_en: Not yet...

t_ch: 那你之后可以试试看。
t_en: You should.

t_ch: 我可是高手。
t_en: I'm a master at it.

t_ch: 是吗？那我们可要找机会对弈一番了。
t_en: Really? Then we should have a match someday.

t_ch: 那并非仙舟象棋，规则却相差不大。列车长见本座对此有兴趣，便将规则传授于我。
t_en: It isn't Xianzhou chess, but the rules are similar. The conductor saw me expressing an interest and graciously decided to teach me.

t_ch: 说起仙舟象棋，本座可说是罗浮上的一等好手，能胜过我的不过寥寥数人。
t_en: Speaking of Xianzhou chess, I'm a bit of an expert — few on the Luofu could ever dream of defeating me.

t_ch: 但你们那位列车长，似乎也不是等闲之辈，与本座对弈数十局，竟是互有胜负。
t_en: But that conductor of yours is no ordinary player either. We played a dozen rounds and ended up in a stalemate.
Hmm...

t_ch: 我往后，大概会常来列车上下棋的……
t_en: Perhaps I should start making regular chess visits to the Express...

t_ch: 你擅长下棋吗？
t_en: Are you good at starchess?

t_ch: 我往后，大概会常来列车上下棋的……
t_en: Perhaps I should start making regular chess visits to the Express...

t_ch: 方才同瓦尔特谈起游云天君，相互分享了很多颇为有趣的传说。
t_en: I was speaking with Mr. Yang about the Cloudstrider, and we shared many fascinating tales.

t_ch: 游云天君的传说？
t_en: Tales about the Cloudstrider?

t_ch: 在仙舟联盟的民间故事中，有很多和游云天君有关的传说。游云天君造虹车、开星轨，遍行天宇、链接瀛寰。
t_en: There are many legends about the Cloudstrider in the Xianzhou Alliance's folktales. The Cloudstrider made a chromatic echelon, created the star rail, and traveled across the universe — connecting many worlds.

t_ch: 祂在银河各处都留下过关于自己的传说，但那些传说也大都真伪不明。
t_en: THEY left many legends throughout the galaxy... though such legends are bound to be contaminated by falsehoods.

t_ch: 游云天君曾将永燃的山火驱逐出地面，使地上的生灵免遭烈焰之苦。
t_en: The Cloudstrider once drove eternal wildfires from the ground, saving life on land from scorching heat.

t_ch: 当本座提起这个故事时，瓦尔特先生却说，在一个叫做「桑尔吉斯克」的地方也有类似的传说…阿基维利将永远在喷发的火山迁移到天上，拯救了万民。
t_en: When I spoke of this, Mr. Yang told me that a similar tale exists at a place called Sangiske... They believe Akivili moved a continuously erupting volcano into the heavens to save the people below.

t_ch: 这样一想，这段传说或许只是古人将自己星系中恒星的起源，附会到游云天君身上了吧。
t_en: Perhaps this legend was simply how ancient peoples accounted for the star in their planetary system — by attributing it to the Cloudstrider.

t_ch: 长知识了。
t_en: You learn something new every day.

t_ch: 这种事情，如果只听仙舟人的传说，或者只听桑尔吉斯克人的传说，恐怕永远也不能得出真相吧？
t_en: If one had heard only of the Xianzhou natives' legend, or the tale of the Sangiske people, it would be impossible to arrive at the truth.

t_ch: 真理往往在兼看兼听之中，若是只能看到自己眼前的东西，那便是播穅眯目，难见大江大河了。
t_en: Truth often lies in seeing and listening to other perspectives. When one sees only what is in front of them, they are blind to the bigger picture.

t_ch: 游云天君的传说……
t_en: Tales about the Cloudstrider...

t_ch: 真理往往在兼看兼听之中，若是只能看到自己眼前的东西，那便是播穅眯目，难见大江大河了。
t_en: Truth often lies in seeing and listening to other perspectives. When one sees only what is in front of them, they are blind to the bigger picture.

t_ch: 说起来，无名客也是个劳神焦思、宵旰忧劳的营生啊…不如来试试本座平常喝的药茶吧？
t_en: Hmm, it would seem that being a Nameless is also a tiresome and difficult pursuit... Oh! You should try the herbal tea I like to drink.

t_ch: 好喝吗？
t_en: Does it taste good?

t_ch: 毕竟是药茶，能好喝到哪儿去？虽说口味是比不上你们列车上的饮品，但效用可是一等一的。
t_en: Have you tried any herbal tea that tastes good? It may not be as palatable as the drinks on the Express, but its efficacy is peerless.

t_ch: 待本座把配方说与你听…这可是我从丹鼎司讨来的秘方，你最好拿笔记一下。
t_en: Let me impart the recipe to you... This is a recipe I specifically acquired from the Alchemy Commission. It's best if you put it down on paper.

t_ch: 上好的狩原毛峰一两、波月莲子三钱、伏冬桑两钱、鳄蝽蜕一钱、草木灰少许。
t_en: One tael of top-tier Hunter Maofeng tea, three maces of lunarescent lotus seeds, two maces of frost mulberries, one mace of shed crocbug skin, and a pinch of plant ash.

t_ch: 然后加水，加糖，糖要加到…加到茶水里再也溶不进一粒糖为止。
t_en: Then, add water and sugar until the water is so saturated that not a single grain of sugar can be dissolved further.

t_ch: 那也太多了！
t_en: That's way too much sugar!

t_ch: 不多不多。一是糖分可以快速让大脑恢复活力，二是这个方子颇为苦涩，不放那么多糖，实在是难以入口……
t_en: No, no — glucose can rapidly revitalize the brain. Furthermore, this recipe is quite bitter. Frankly, the tea is hard to swallow unless it has that much sugar...

t_ch: 你按这个方子先调一杯。要是喝不惯…那便是糖的问题。本座下次给你带仙舟的糖粉来。
t_en: You should at least try making one cup. If you don't like the flavor... then it must be the sugar. I'll bring some Xianzhou powdered sugar next time.

t_ch: 需要药茶的配方吗？
t_en: Do you need the herbal tea recipe?

t_ch: 你按这个方子先调一杯。要是喝不惯…那便是糖的问题。本座下次给你带仙舟的糖粉来。
t_en: You should at least try making one cup. If you don't like the flavor... then it must be the sugar. I'll bring some Xianzhou powdered sugar next time.

t_ch: 罗浮横遭大难，本与星穹列车无关，但列车组还是仗义相助，帮罗浮安然渡过此劫。蒙受了星穹列车这么大的恩情，却一直没来得及答谢你们。
t_en: The sudden catastrophe that the Luofu suffered was unrelated to the Astral Express, yet your Crew gallantly provided assistance and helped us through this trial. The Astral Express has helped us so much, but I still haven't managed to thank you.

t_ch: 正巧，最近本座听闻有个商会进口了几块上好的宝石，便收入囊中。今次就将这几块宝石赠予你，权当是答谢了。
t_en: It just happens that I heard a merchant guild recently imported some premium precious stones, and I proceeded to acquire them. I would like to gift them to you today to show my gratitude.

t_ch: 诗曰：「无瑕胜玉美，至洁过冰清。」这宝石与你，最是相配不过了。
t_en: It is said that nothing is as impeccable as green jadeite, and nothing is as pure as white jade. These precious stones are a fitting gift for you.

t_ch: 一直没来得及答谢……
t_en: I haven't had the chance to thank you...

t_ch: 诗曰：「无瑕胜玉美，至洁过冰清。」这宝石与你，最是相配不过了。
t_en: It is said that nothing is as impeccable as green jadeite, and nothing is as pure as white jade. These precious stones are a fitting gift for you.

t_ch: 唷！是{NICKNAME}呀~这是我第一次造访星穹列车，怎么不见你带我四处转转？这可不符合无名客的待客之道啊~
t_en: Hey! {NICKNAME}! It's my first time on the Astral Express. I've heard quite a bit about the hospitality of the Nameless. Shouldn't you be showing me around?

t_ch: 疏忽了，抱歉…
t_en: You're right. My apologies...

t_ch: 哎呀，我就开个玩笑，你别露出那副表情嘛。
t_en: Ahh, I'm just messing with you — lighten up!

t_ch: 你这不是挺自在的嘛？
t_en: You seem quite at home already.

t_ch: 还是被你看出来了~咱们都是熟人了，相互之间也不必太客气，是吧？
t_en: Haha, so you've noticed. Well, we're hardly strangers, better to skip the formalities, right?

t_ch: 要说登上过这趟星穹列车的公司员工，我肯定不是第一个。据说「开拓」阿基维利还在世的那会儿，公司和无名客之间还有不少往来合作。
t_en: I'm definitely not the first IPC staff member to board the Astral Express. I've heard that the IPC and the Nameless cooperated frequently when Akivili the Trailblazer was still around.

t_ch: 我还听说，董事会甚至考虑过花大价钱包下星穹列车，让它成为公司业务的一环…但所有的提议都被果断拒绝了。
t_en: I've also heard that the board once considered spending a fortune to buy out the Astral Express and to make it part of their business... However, all their offers were quickly declined.

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你哦，我觉得拒绝很明智。
t_en: Just between you and me, I think declining the offers was a smart choice.

t_ch: 对这个宇宙而言，资本必不可少——但假如世界上所有的事物都被资本收进囊中，那这个故事就一点都不浪漫了…你说是吧？
t_en: Capital definitely plays a critical role in the universe... but if everything in the world revolved around it, then life would be a lot less romantic... don't you think?

t_ch: 原来车厢里面长这样~
t_en: So this is what the inside of the Astral Express looks like...

t_ch: 话说回来，上车需要办什么手续吗？签合同？交押金？保证书总有吧？
t_en: Say, aren't there any forms to fill out for boarding the express? No contract to be signed? How about a deposit? Tsk, at least tell me there's a guarantee...

t_ch: …都没有？嚯，有趣…纯粹建立在「私人信用」之上的合作关系，居然能开展得如此顺利…真不简单！
t_en: Nothing...? Hmm, interesting... So it's all based on interpersonal credit? Huh, I'm amazed everything runs on something so fragile!

t_ch: 欸，{NICKNAME}，我问你，星穹列车上是不是种了「镰叶紫菀草」？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, are there any Asters on the Astral Express?

t_ch: 我不知道啊…
t_en: I have no idea...

t_ch: 对哦，一般的乘客也不会特别关注身边的绿植是什么品种的…算啦~
t_en: Hmm, I suppose most passengers don't pay much attention to the plant life around them... Never mind⁓

t_ch: 什么草？
t_en: What are they?

t_ch: 「镰叶紫菀草」，就是……
t_en: Asters, you know, Asters...

t_ch: 咳，算啦算啦，看表情就知道，你对这事完全没了解~
t_en: Ahem, never mind. I can tell from the look on your face that you have no idea what I'm talking about.

t_ch: 告诉你吧，「镰叶紫菀草」会散发出一种特别的气味，人类的鼻子几乎察觉不到。但账账对这种气味特别敏感——瞧！
t_en: Asters are a type of plant that release a unique scent — barely noticeable to humans. However, Numby is extremely sensitive to it — look!

t_ch: （愉快的叫声）
t_en: (Joyful Noises)

t_ch: 我听专家说，对于特定的生物而言，这种植物散发的味道就像一大锅被熬化的蜜糖，一般的小动物根本把持不住。
t_en: The experts say that to certain creatures, the scent these plants release is like a big pot of caramelized honey. Most small creatures lose their minds over it.

t_ch: 但是「镰叶紫菀草」的叶片其实含有剧烈的神经毒素，假如有动物循着味道来到它的面前，然后咬上一口它的叶片……
t_en: The bad side is that Aster leaves actually contain an extremely strong neurotoxin. If an animal were to find one by following its scent and take a bite of it...

t_ch: 哎呀，真是恶毒的植物啊~账账，你可不许乱吃东西哦？
t_en: Ah! What a terrible plant! Numby, don't eat anything you're not supposed to, okay!?

t_ch: （乖巧的叫声）
t_en: (Expresses obedience)

t_ch: 账账，乖，别乱跑~
t_en: Numby, behave! Don't keep running around~

t_ch: 真的有人会在车厢里种「镰叶紫菀草」吗？胆子可真大啊~
t_en: Is someone really growing Asters on the Express? That's quite a bold thing to do!

t_ch: 我盯着你呢，账账！可不许乱吃东西哦。
t_en: I have my eyes on you, Numby! Make sure you don't eat anything you're not supposed to.

t_ch: 嘿，是{NICKNAME}呀？又见面啦~
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}? We meet again!

t_ch: 这里实在太无聊了，我正在练习心算打发时间~正好，要不你来出题考我吧？越难越好。
t_en: I'm getting so bored here that I've started practicing mental math to pass the time... Since you're here, why don't you test me? Make it a hard one.

t_ch: <unbreak>7523</unbreak>减去<unbreak>2956</unbreak>…
t_en: <unbreak>7523</unbreak> minus <unbreak>2956</unbreak>...

t_ch: <unbreak>4567</unbreak>。
t_en: <unbreak>4567</unbreak>.

t_ch: <unbreak>317</unbreak>乘以<unbreak>226</unbreak>…
t_en: <unbreak>317</unbreak> multiplied by <unbreak>226</unbreak>...

t_ch: <unbreak>71642</unbreak>。
t_en: <unbreak>71,642</unbreak>.

t_ch: 三月七除以帕姆…
t_en: March 7th divided by Pom-Pom...

t_ch: 是要拿他们俩的可爱指数来计算吗？那这道题也太难了，我可算不出啊~
t_en: Huh? Hmm, we'd have to assign them both a numerical value based on a shared characteristic, like... cuteness? But in that case, we'd be talking astronomical values... Ugh, this one's too hard — I can't do it!

t_ch: 还是问我道正经的数学题吧。
t_en: Can we go with a normal math question?

t_ch: <unbreak>8182</unbreak>减去<unbreak>6479</unbreak>…
t_en: <unbreak>8182</unbreak> minus <unbreak>6479</unbreak>...

t_ch: <unbreak>1703</unbreak>。
t_en: <unbreak>1703</unbreak>.

t_ch: <unbreak>597</unbreak>乘以<unbreak>464</unbreak>…
t_en: <unbreak>597</unbreak> multiplied by <unbreak>464</unbreak>...

t_ch: <unbreak>277008</unbreak>。
t_en: <unbreak>277,008</unbreak>.

t_ch: 这题够简单的，你还挺懂手下留情的嘛？
t_en: These are so easy. You really are gentle with people, aren't you?

t_ch: 来，再考我一道题吧，这次多上点强度~
t_en: Come on! One more — and make it challenging!

t_ch: <unbreak>28629151</unbreak>的五次方根是？
t_en: What is the fifth root of <unbreak>28,629,151</unbreak>?

t_ch: <unbreak>31</unbreak>。
t_en: <unbreak>31</unbreak>.

t_ch: 把<unbreak>73153</unbreak>分解质因数。
t_en: Prime factors of <unbreak>73,153</unbreak>.

t_ch: <unbreak>191</unbreak>乘以<unbreak>383</unbreak>。
t_en: <unbreak>191</unbreak> multiplied by <unbreak>383</unbreak>.

t_ch: C5H9NO4是什么的化学式？
t_en: What is C5H9NO4 the molecular formula of?

t_ch: 等会儿等会儿，你这分明是化学题吧？
t_en: Hold on a second... That's obviously a chemistry problem.

t_ch: 答案我倒是知道啦——「谷氨酸」，对吧？
t_en: ...but that doesn't stop me from knowing the answer! It's glutamic acid, isn't it?

t_ch: 有点意思！能一下想出这么有趣的题目，看来你也不是等闲之辈嘛？
t_en: This is fun! I'm impressed by your ability to come up with such interesting questions on the spot!

t_ch: 很久没遇上像你这么有趣的人了。下次有机会，我们再好好交流交流~
t_en: I haven't met anyone as interesting as you for a while. We should talk more when we get another chance!

t_ch: 一二三四五~
t_en: One, two, three, four, five...

t_ch: 有句话是怎么说的来着…哦对了，「学好数理化，入职公司都不怕！」
t_en: What was that saying again...? Oh, right, "Math, physics, and chemistry are your key to the future!"

t_ch: 哪怕已经进入职场了，也不要忘记持续学习哦~
t_en: Don't forget to continue learning new stuff even after you've found a job!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，列车上有吃的吗？账账好像有点饿了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, is there any food on the Express? I think Numby's hungry.

t_ch: 给账账喂「奇巧零食」。
t_en: Feed Numby Trick Snack.

t_ch: （委屈的声音）
t_en: (Dissatisfied noises)

t_ch: 哎呀，看来账账不太喜欢这个呢……
t_en: Hmm, afraid Numby's not a fan...

t_ch: 我还是带它去餐车吧…走了，账账~
t_en: I'm just gonna take them to the dining carriage... Let's go, Numby!

t_ch: 给账账喂「虚无硬片」。
t_en: Feed Numby Hard Chip of Nihility.

t_ch: （开心的声音）
t_en: (Happy noises)

t_ch: 喔，看来账账很喜欢这个哦！
t_en: Oh? Seems like Numby really likes that!

t_ch: 不过它好像还没吃饱…我还是带它去餐车吧。走了，账账~
t_en: Still looks kinda hungry, though... I'm just gonna take them to the dining carriage. Let's go, Numby!

t_ch: 给账账喂「固态净水」。
t_en: Feed Numby Solid Water.

t_ch: （疑惑的声音）
t_en: (Confused noises)

t_ch: 呃…它是饿了，不是渴了~
t_en: Uhh... Numby's hungry, not thirsty.

t_ch: 我还是带它去餐车吧…走了，账账~
t_en: I'm just gonna take them to the dining carriage... Let's go, Numby!

t_ch: 账账，饿了吗？
t_en: Numby, you hungry?

t_ch: 话说回来，餐车在哪来着……
t_en: By the way... Where's the dining carriage?

t_ch: 还是去问问列车长吧。
t_en: I should ask the conductor.

t_ch: （托帕好像在打电话……）
t_en: (Seems like Topaz is on the phone...)

t_ch: …啊？你说什么？开盘就跌了百分之十五？
t_en: Huh...? Are you serious? <unbreak>15%</unbreak> drop on opening?

t_ch: 吁…有鬼。别急，等我先派人去了解下情况。
t_en: Hmm... strange. Don't worry, I'll have someone check it out.

t_ch: 杰奇，能听见吗？帮我打电话给理事长……
t_en: Jackie, can you hear me? I need you to call the chairman for me...

t_ch: 哎呀，当然是图拉斯制药的卡洛斯理事长！别问这么业余的问题好不好？
t_en: Ugh! Chairman Carlos of Tulas Pharmaceuticals, obviously! Knock it off with the amateur questions!

t_ch: …「翡翠」找我？请她稍等，我5分钟后联系她。
t_en: ...Jade is looking for me? I'll contact her in five minutes.

t_ch: …巴里也有事？让他转备用线路，我现在有急事处理，晚点再回。
t_en: Barry needs me too...? Put him on another line — I have something urgent to take care of. I'll contact him later.

t_ch: （还是别去打扰她了……）
t_en: (Let's not disturb her...)

t_ch: 买入。对，现在就买。
t_en: Buy. Yes, just buy it.

t_ch: 小插曲而已~别管它。
t_en: Market hiccup — don't worry about it.

t_ch: （托帕还在打电话……）
t_en: (Topaz is still on the phone...)

t_ch: （公司职员…真辛苦啊。）
t_en: (Having a job at the IPC sure is a lot of work...)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！快来快来，我有东西要给你~
t_en: {NICKNAME}, come here! Hurry! I have something for you!

t_ch: 为了感谢星穹列车一直以来对我们业务的鼎力支持，「战略投资部」决定赠送给各位一样礼物。
t_en: As a gesture of appreciation for the Astral Express' continuous support for our business, the Strategic Investment Department has prepared a gift for the Crew.

t_ch: 拿着吧，别客气~处理客户关系最重要的一点，就是要学会礼尚往来——希望诸位未来也能继续支持我们的工作！
t_en: There's no need to be modest! The most important thing about consumer relations is the bond between us. I hope everyone will continue to support our work in the future!

t_ch: 重要的是礼尚往来…
t_en: What's important is reciprocation in kind...

t_ch: 你们的下一个目的地是哪？要是我正好有同事在那儿，我可以提前帮各位打声招呼，好好招待下你们。
t_en: Where are you guys headed next? If a colleague of mine happens to be there, I can give them a heads-up and have them show you guys around.

t_ch: 别有负担，这都是最寻常的礼节，算不上卖人情。公司也不是人人都那么功利啦~
t_en: That's what friends are for — don't be a stranger. Not everyone in the IPC is driven by pure ambition, y'know⁓

t_ch: 这片星空……
t_en: These stars...

t_ch: 谢谢你的邀请，{NICKNAME}。我在这里可以暂时忘掉繁杂的政务，还能欣赏到如此美妙的星空…我很满足。
t_en: Thank you for inviting me, {NICKNAME} — I'll be able to get away from the complexities of politics here. Being surrounded by these beautiful stars... it's such a joy.

t_ch: 虽然一起经历了这么多，但其实我对星穹列车的背景还知之甚少。有机会的话，我很想多来做几次客。
t_en: Although we've been through so much together, I actually know very little about the Astral Express. I'd like to start visiting more frequently if the opportunity arises.

t_ch: 我其实也不太了解列车…
t_en: I actually don't know much about the Express either...

t_ch: 这样吗？嗯，说的也是…就像三月，她似乎并不记得自己的过去——列车组的各位，感觉背景都相当神秘呢。
t_en: Really? Hmm, I suppose you wouldn't... That reminds me of March — she can't remember her past. Feels like everyone in the Express Crew has a mysterious background.

t_ch: 欢迎你随时光临。
t_en: You're always welcome here.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。希望我能有时间多多了解列车上的各位。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. Hopefully, I'll have more time to get to know the Crew.

t_ch: 我很喜欢盯着这窗外的宇宙看。这景象既迷幻又深邃，还夹杂着些许浪漫。
t_en: I really enjoy staring out into the universe. What a vast, mesmerizing, romantic view.

t_ch: 可能的话，我希望有朝一日，贝洛伯格的孩子们也能像我一样，近距离地感受星空。
t_en: I hope that one day the children of Belobog will have the same opportunity to witness the stars up close.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，有事的话就尽管去忙吧？不用在意我，我只想再多看一会儿这外面的景色。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, please, don't mind me. Feel free to go about your business. I think I'll stay here and enjoy the view a while longer.

t_ch: 战士，冒险家，大守护者…同一片星空之下，我们人类扮演着各自的角色，过着各自的人生。
t_en: Warriors, adventurers, supreme guardians — we all gaze upon the same stars, the same sky... each with our own roles to play, our own independent lives to live.

t_ch: 我们为了生存所付出的努力，或许微小…但绝不卑微。
t_en: The work we put in to survive may seem small in the grand scheme of things... but that doesn't mean it's not important.
